meta.title: EchoLog
meta.description: Case study of EchoLog: a multi-tenant customer feedback SaaS built with React 19, Express 5, Prisma, PostgreSQL (Supabase). Screaming architecture, 606 tests, 45 PRs over 3 months.

header.title: EchoLog
header.short: Multi-tenant customer feedback SaaS - boards, upvoting, comments, workspaces, board approval, email invitations. Full-stack TypeScript with React 19, Express 5, Prisma, PostgreSQL (Supabase). ~20,000 lines, pnpm monorepo.

summary.heading: Summary
summary.text: EchoLog is a multi-tenant customer feedback SaaS - a Canny.io alternative - that allows product teams to collect, organize, and act on user feedback through boards, upvoting, comments, and workspace management.

problem.heading: The problem
problem.text: Product teams need a centralized place to gather user feedback, prioritize features based on community votes, and communicate status updates - without building it from scratch or paying for expensive tools like Canny. Most alternatives are either overpriced, locked into proprietary ecosystems, or lack role-based access and multi-workspace isolation.

solution.heading: The solution
list: pnpm monorepo with 4 packages (server, web, shared, e2e) and shared Zod contracts as single source of truth: 34 schemas typing both frontend and backend simultaneously via z.infer<>.
Screaming architecture across 10 business domains: auth, posts, votes, comments, boards, workspaces, notifications, email, infra. Each domain exposes router, controller, service, and Prisma queries.
Manual JWT (crypto.createHmac + base64url, ~50 lines) stored in httpOnly cookies with sameSite=none. No jsonwebtoken: zero dependencies with recurring CVEs, zero prototype pollution attacks.

Multi-tenant isolation via composite Prisma keys (@@unique([workspaceId, slug])) instead of global slugs. Membership middleware closure resolves workspace from params, body, or query at any route depth.
~606 tests across Vitest (unit), React Testing Library (components), and Supertest (integration). CI with GitHub Actions and real PostgreSQL in a service container. No database mocking.
45 PRs merged over 3 months with stacked feature branches ≤200 lines each. Per-endpoint rate limiting with granular configuration. 12+ documented bugs post-release.

architecture.heading: Architecture & decisions
architecture.text: EchoLog follows screaming architecture on the backend - each business domain is a top-level folder that 'screams' its purpose without revealing the framework. Key decisions with explicit tradeoffs:
list: JWT without jsonwebtoken: crypto.createHmac('sha256', secret) + base64url encoding (~50 lines). HS256 is sufficient for a single auth server. Avoiding jsonwebtoken eliminates its recurring CVEs and 70kB footprint. If we scale to multiple auth servers, we'd migrate to RS256 with a dedicated library.
Zod as single source of truth: 34 shared schemas between frontend and backend via shared/. DTO types derived with z.infer<>. Zero desync between layers. Tradeoff: Zod 3 has fewer plugins than Joi.

Membership middleware factory: closure that captures allowed roles and resolves workspace from any URL depth (postId → boardId → workspaceId). Protected routes declared with requireWorkspaceMember(['ADMIN', 'OWNER']). One liner.
Composite Prisma keys: @@unique([workspaceId, slug]) isolates namespaces per tenant without separate PostgreSQL schemas. Every query includes workspaceId scoping. Zero cross-tenant leakage.

Stacked feature branches: 5 chained PRs instead of one 1000+ line PR. Each diff ≤ 200 lines. Reviews take minutes, not hours. Conflicts surface early. Post-release merge confirmed this strategy saves days of debugging.
Strategic rate limiting: per-endpoint limits (auth 20/15min, votes 30/1min, invitations 20/15min, public 30/1min). Configured in the domain router, not globally. Disabled in tests (NODE_ENV=test).

implementation.heading: Featured implementation
implementation.hookDescription: Express middleware that extracts workspace ID from params, body, or query, looks up the user's membership in a single Prisma query, and optionally enforces a minimum role. Protects every workspace-scoped endpoint: boards, posts, votes, comments, invitations, and settings. Consistent authorization across the entire backend with one function.

accessibility.heading: Accessibility
accessibility.lead: EchoLog was audited systematically (P0→P3) for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all interfaces.
list: Skip link, semantic landmarks, and proper heading hierarchy throughout the application.
Form errors announced via aria-live regions, input labels with htmlFor, and aria-describedby for helper text.
Keyboard navigation preserved across all interactive components: boards, posts, votes, comments, and modals.
Dynamic page titles updated on route changes for screen reader context.
Focus management in dialogs (trap + restore), mobile sidebar (auto-focus + Escape), and interactive elements.
prefers-reduced-motion respected - all CSS animations respect the system setting.

performance.heading: Metrics & results
list: ~20,000 lines of code, 216 files, ~60 React components, 48 API endpoints, 11 Prisma models, 34 Zod schemas.
~606 tests (64 files), ~90 commits, 45 PRs merged over 3 months of development, 18 SDD phases completed.
React Query optimistic updates on votes: instantaneous UI feedback with automatic rollback on 409 conflicts.
List virtualization with @tanstack/react-virtual gated on mobile OR >20 items: large boards scroll at 60fps even with hundreds of posts.
Debounced search (300ms) prevents excessive API calls during fast typing. Cursor-based pagination with 'Load more' pattern.
Granular per-endpoint rate limiting. OKLCH design tokens via PostCSS on the frontend. Cache headers on API responses.

challenges.heading: Real bugs & debugging
list: CI failures from 3 root causes: Prisma connection pool not disconnecting between suites, rate limiter state leaking across tests, and PgBouncer prepared statement errors. Each required a different fix strategy.
Notification bell dropdown rendering outside viewport on mobile: bottom-full on a sticky header positions the dropdown below the viewport. Fix: switch to top-full when the header is sticky.
Silent 404: resendVerification service existed but the route and controller were never wired. The frontend called an endpoint that returned an unhelpful 404. Missing wire-up in the auth router.

Post-release merge conflict: a complex merge dropped files that existed on both branches. CI didn't catch the loss because existing tests didn't cover those files. Lesson: tests must run on merge results, not just individual branches.

improvements.heading: What could be improved
list: WebSockets (Socket.IO or SSE) for real-time vote count and notification updates without polling.
Public workspace discovery feed with popularity ranking and a read-only lobby with no sign-up required.
Cursor-based pagination for notifications: currently loads all notifications on dropdown open. With 50+ notifications, rendering degrades.
Expand E2E coverage with Playwright: critical flows for multi-tenant auth, board approval, and invitations are the priority candidates.
